title: Embed viewer component with responsive viewport controls
purpose: Displays embedded content in iframes with customizable viewports and error handling for storybook embeds.
module_context: Exports a React component EmbedViewer that renders embedded content with viewport controls and validation for storybook embeds.

steps.content: Initialize state for loading status, error state, viewport mode, custom dimensions, and storybook validation.
Set up a callback to refresh the iframe content by reloading its source.
Validate storybook embeds when the component mounts or when storybook parameters change.
Determine if the embed is a storybook and whether it should be framed based on viewport selection.
Generate CSS styles for the iframe container based on the selected viewport or custom dimensions.
Render the title bar with viewport controls and refresh button.
Display validation error message if the storybook story is not found.
Show custom input fields for width and height when custom viewport is selected.
Reset loading and error states.
Reload the iframe source by setting its src attribute to its current value.
Abort previous validation request if component unmounts.
Fetch validation status for storybook embeds using the port and story ID.
Update validation state with the response data.
Set error message if validation fails.

methods.name: getFrameStyle
methods.normalized_params: (): { width: number; height: number; radius: string; border: number } | null

steps.content: Return custom dimensions if viewport is set to custom.
Return preset dimensions for mobile, tablet, or desktop viewports.
Return null if viewport is set to full screen.

methods.name: ViewportButton
methods.normalized_params: (props: { mode: ViewportMode; title: string; children: React.ReactNode }): React.ReactElement

steps.content: Render a button with styling that changes based on whether it's the active viewport.
Set viewport mode to custom or toggle between preset modes when clicked.
Show custom input fields when custom viewport is selected.
